properties.remark: THE STAGE HEIGHT ON A USGS GAGE ON THE ALLATOONA CREEK AT STILESBORO ROAD NEAR MARS HILL REACHED FLOOD STAGE OF 13 FEET. THE CREEK IS CONTINUING TO RISE SLOWLY. MINOR FLOODING IS OCCURRING IN THE WOODLANDS AND FIELDS NEAR THE CREEK.
A REPORT OF AROUND TEN TREES DOWN NEAR APPLEWOOD COURT AND BRAITHWOOD ROAD

THE STAGE HEIGHT ON A USGS STREAM GAGE ON THE BIG CREEK AT KIMBALL BRIDGE ROAD NEAR ALPHARETTA REACHED FLOOD STAGE OF 7 FEET. THE CREEK WAS CONTINUING TO RISE. MINOR FLOODING WAS OCCURRING IN WOODLANDS...FIELDS AND PORTIONS OF THE BIG CREEK GREENWAY.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN NEAR APPLEWOOD COURT AND BRAITHWOOD ROAD...COSMOS DR...JASMINE CT AND CANNA RIDGE CIR.

THE STAGE HEIGHT ON A USGS GAGE ON EUHARLEE CREEK AT US HWY 278 OR WEST ELM STREET IN ROCKMART REACHED FLOOD STAGE OF 9 FEET. THE CREEK IS CONTINUING TO RISE SLOWLY. MINOR FLOODING IS OCCURING ON PORTIONS OF THE WAYSIDE PARK. SECTIONS OF THE SIDEWALK ALONG WATER STREET IS FLOODED.

THE STAGE HEIGHT ON A USGS GAGE ON THE LITTLE RIVER AT WOODSTOCK REACHED FLOOD STAGE OF 11 FEET. THE CREEK IS CONTINUING TO RISE. MINOR FLOODING IS OCCURRING IN THE WOODLANDS AND FIELDS UPSTREAM AND DOWNSTREAM FROM THE GAGE AT OLD GEORGIA HWY 5. A PUBLIC BOAT RAMP JUST DOWNSTREAM FROM THE GAGE IS UNDER WATER. LOW LYING AREAS OF THE SOUTH CHEROKEE RECREATION PARK HAVE BEGUN TO FLOOD.

A BRIEF EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN FAR SOUTHERN PULASKI COUNTY AT THE 1600 BLOCK OF PINEVIEW HWY. THE TORNADO TRAVELED JUST 0.2 MILES. APPROX 20 SMALL AND MEDIUM PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED AS WELL AS A VERY LARGE OAK LIMB WHICH FELL ONTO A BRICK OUTBUILDING. MINOR DAMAGE DONE TO THE ROOF. A WELLHOUSE COVERING WAS ALSO BLOWN OFF. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE RATED AT 85 MPH WITH A MAX PATH WIDTH OF 50 YDS.

ANOTHER EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EASTERN PULASKI COUNTY ON RALIEGH COBB RD AND TRAVELED EAST NORTHEAST FOR 2.2 MILES. A TIN COVERED BARN WITH 4 INCH LOOSELY ATTACHED CONCRETE FOOTINGS WAS BLOWN OVER. MINOR DAMAGE TO THE ROOF OF A HOME. TWO TREES WERE DOWNED 2 MILES NORTHEAST OF THAT LOCATION NEAR MIDWAY CHURCH RD AND BILL BROWN RD.
ROOF OF BARN BLOWN OFF ON RALEIGH COBB RD.

A BRIEF EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF LOG CABIN ROAD AND ANTIOCH CHURCH ROAD AND TRAVELED JUST 0.3 MILES THROUGH THE INTERSECTION. ABOUT 10 TREES WERE DOWNED NARROWLY MISSING HOMES AND CARS. MAX WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 95 MPH...MAX PATH WIDTH 120 YARRDS.
SKYWARN COORDINATOR REPORTED POWERLINES DOWN AND TREES DOWN AND BLOCKING THE ROAD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF LOG CABIN ROAD AND ANTIOCH CHURCH ROAD NEAR EASTMAN.

A FEW DUPLEX HOMES IN A LOW LYING AREA OFF THE 900 BLOCK OF EAST FURLOW STREET WERE FLOODED WHEN A NEARBY DRAINAGE DITCH OVERFLOWED. SEVERAL PEOPLE WERE EVACUATED FROM THEIR HOMES. AROUND A DOZEN CARS WERE INUNDATED BY THE FLOOD WATERS UP TO THEIR DOOR HANDLES. THE FLOOD WATERS COVERED A PORTION OF EAST FURLOW STREET.
